chats.text: I was made aware that the two lost days in February had a meaningful impact on our Q1 runway, right?
For a projection standpoint we're carrying a delta relative to target that hasn't moved in our favor, right? 😅
I do think there's value in leaning into any levers and capturing incremental gains. I was made aware of the downstream implications already.
Your golf reservation got cancelled Charles. Historical speaking, your attendance patterns align with Q3 attainment targets.
I was made aware that you're the only person in this company who's seen my full calendar. I owe you a drink, right?
Your attendance is historically aligned to Q3 attainment, right? But seriously, can we reschedule? I was made aware of this meeting two minutes ago.
It's an important element that full attainment by EOD is no longer feasible. Still, I believe there's value in reducing the delta as much as possible.
That makes sense — we should capture whatever incremental gains we can to move closer to target before quarter close.
You're the only person who's seen my calendar in full. I was made aware you wanted a drink earlier — mine is on the house, right?
At this point, the numbers are less of a projection and more of a conclusion, right? How do we lean into any total levers for incremental gains?
You know I was made aware that Ryan's absence creates downstream applications. Can someone please summarize what he meant by 'downstream' without the corporate verb noun filter?
I was made aware that we're carrying a fairly meaningful gap. right?
the runway simply won't allow us adequate time to reach our performance objective. right? 🕰️
{NAME}, I was made aware you were in the room today. Consider this a heads up on downstream implications. right?
I think there's still value in reducing that delta as much as possible. right?
I was made aware you're recalibrating on the potential downstream applications of your absence, Ryan. Historically speaking, that's when the numbers get wonky.
how do we lean into any total levers to capture whatever incremental gains we can? right?
{NAME}, I was made aware that full attainment by EOD is no longer feasible. Still trying to move closer to the target though.
for a sort of projection standpoint, there's simply no version where our attainment discrepancy closes today. right? 📉
I was made aware you're carrying a fairly meaningful gap, Braxton. I'm recalibrating on your downstream applications. right?
I believe it's essentially correct that we can't reclaim the two lost days from February. right? 🤷‍♂️
I was made aware you believe full attainment by EOD is no longer feasible, Braxton. That's historically speaking the most common position we've taken.
we're getting at here that the numbers are less of a projection and more of a conclusion. right?
I was made aware you're getting at here that full attainment is no longer feasible, Charles. I think there's still value in reducing the delta.
{NAME}, at this point the numbers are less of a projection and more of a conclusion. Lean into whatever total levers you have.
while full attainment by EOD is no longer feasible, I do think there's value in continuing to reduce that delta as much as possible. right? 💡
Just circling back to make sure I'm on the same page about the February runway gap, right?
So my golf tee time for the 12th was bumped up from 3pm to 4:30. Attainment shortfall means my handicap is now officially 'corporate'.
I'm circling back to clarify that by 'downstream applications' I don't mean any actual downstream work, right?
Ryan just looked at me when he said 'headwinds.' I think he's talking about wind. Or a metaphor for wind.
you ever feel like your team time prevalence is just... predestined? Like the calendar was written by someone who hated weekends?
just to confirm, the February shortfall is now baked into Q1's baseline? right?
{NAME}, did you see Ryan's calendar before he joined? I was like 'oh, he's probably just here to rubber stamp' right?
the delta between us and Q1 targets is now a conclusion, not an estimate. Right? So let's act accordingly.
Charles — you're the one who 'historically speaking' gets late. How do we make that time prevalence count?
attainment shortfall is a construct, but it's real enough to make me want a mid-afternoon beverage right now.
{NAME}, I was made aware that you're tracking the team time prevalence. Please include an escalation vector in your next update.
I was made aware that 'no version of reality' is a conclusion, not an estimate. Does that mean we're done with projections?
Ryan — you joined 'just to quickly recalibrate.' That's not a call, that's a ghost in the machine. Right?
any incremental gains we can capture are now the baseline for next quarter. So we're starting from 'done.' Right.
{NAME}, I was made aware that Ryan's absence has downstream applications — like this one, where the chat is getting weird.
the Q1 runway isn't just 'not allowing adequate time' — it's a deceleration that started in February. Right?
Braxton, I was made aware you're the one who flags these things. So your flag is now the conclusion.
we were 'effectively unable to reclaim' two days — that's corporate for 'our golf season ate our Q1.'
Ryan — you said full attainment is 'no longer feasible.' That's a conclusion, right? So are we just... acknowledging it?
